narrative:
DRIVER
USD leads all CSM ranks at 100 D1 and 95 H4 in a Risk-Off Medium regime, driven by hawkish Fed repricing, US 10Y yields at 4.667 up 0.95%, and geopolitical safe-haven demand crushing risk currencies. EURUSD D1+H4 Strong Sell is the cleanest expression; USDCHF H4 Strong Buy is amplified by the -0.85 EURUSD correlation confirming CHF weakness absorbs no safe-haven bid here, and AUDJPY H4 Strong Sell at -6 is reinforced by AUDUSD+AUDJPY 0.90 correlation dragging both legs lower simultaneously. GBP H4 CSM diverges 40 points above D1 (100 vs 60), the largest acceleration in the dataset, flagging short-term GBP resilience that conflicts with cooling UK inflation news — correlation with GBPJPY at +0.89 means any GBP fade hits GBPJPY hard and fast.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: GBPJPY H4 momentum 81 and rising sharply, NZDJPY H4 63 and accelerating
Fading: USDCAD D1 strong at 76 but H4 collapsed to 48, AUDUSD both TFs falling with H4 at 45

WATCH
US 10Y yield extension through 4.70 triggers USDJPY topside resumption, reversing current H4 neutral read toward D1 Buy alignment
UK CPI follow-through data release hits GBPUSD directly; a second consecutive downside print confirms D1 Sell dominates the H4 100 CSM spike and validates GBPJPY short entry
WTI Oil at 102.6 down 4.77% extending lower removes CAD commodity support, accelerating USDCAD toward D1 Buy target and undermining CADJPY which already shows H4 fading momentum

themes.theme: US Dollar strength from hawkish Fed repricing and yield support
Rising US Treasury yields limiting precious metals and equity upside
Geopolitical risk premium from US-Iran tensions supporting safe havens
UK inflation cooling faster than expected weighing on GBP
Risk-off sentiment driving equity selloff and flight to safety
Energy prices elevated supporting commodity currencies volatility
BoC rate cut expectations strengthening from cooling inflation
ECB in hold pattern pending June decision amid inflation persistence
Emerging market currencies under pressure from higher USD yields and geopolitical risk

key_observation: US Dollar is the dominant outperformer driven by hawkish Fed repricing reflected in Treasury yields hitting post-crisis highs, combined with safe-haven demand from escalating US-Iran geopolitical tensions. Equities and risk assets are selling off sharply while safe-haven currencies JPY and CHF are bid. Meanwhile, major counterparts like GBP and EUR are under pressure from domestic softness in UK inflation and Euro area uncertainty, while emerging markets face dual headwinds from USD strength and risk aversion.
watch: FOMC Minutes release expected today will be critical for confirming or challenging recent hawkish repricing. US-Iran diplomatic developments could trigger sharp reversals if tensions ease. Oil price trajectory above USD 107 remains inflationary wildcard. DXY resistance at 99.44 and USD/JPY near 159.00 are key technical levels. Monitor for any ECB signals on June policy.
